root_text.an8.2:1.1: “Aṭṭhime, bhikkhave, hetū aṭṭha paccayā ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattanti.
root_text.an8.2:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satthāraṁ upanissāya viharati aññataraṁ vā garuṭṭhāniyaṁ sabrahmacāriṁ, yatthassa tibbaṁ hirottappaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti pemañca gāravo ca.
root_text.an8.2:1.4: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo hetu paṭhamo paccayo ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:2.1: So taṁ satthāraṁ upanissāya viharanto aññataraṁ vā garuṭṭhāniyaṁ sabrahmacāriṁ, yatthassa tibbaṁ hirottappaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti pemañca gāravo ca, te kālena kālaṁ upasaṅkamitvā paripucchati paripañhati:
root_text.an8.2:2.3: Tassa te āyasmanto avivaṭañceva vivaranti, anuttānīkatañca uttānīkaronti, anekavihitesu ca kaṅkhāṭhāniyesu dhammesu kaṅkhaṁ paṭivinodenti.
root_text.an8.2:2.4: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo hetu dutiyo paccayo ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:3.1: So taṁ dhammaṁ sutvā dvayena vūpakāsena sampādeti—kāyavūpakāsena ca cittavūpakāsena ca.
root_text.an8.2:3.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo hetu tatiyo paccayo ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:4.1: Sīlavā hoti, pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī, samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu.
root_text.an8.2:4.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, catuttho hetu catuttho paccayo ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:5.1: Bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo. Ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpāssa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an8.2:5.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamo hetu pañcamo paccayo ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:6.1: Āraddhavīriyo viharati akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ upasampadāya, thāmavā daḷhaparakkamo anikkhittadhuro kusalesu dhammesu.
root_text.an8.2:6.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, chaṭṭho hetu chaṭṭho paccayo ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:7.1: Saṅghagato kho pana anānākathiko hoti atiracchānakathiko.
root_text.an8.2:7.2: Sāmaṁ vā dhammaṁ bhāsati paraṁ vā ajjhesati ariyaṁ vā tuṇhībhāvaṁ nātimaññati.
root_text.an8.2:7.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, sattamo hetu sattamo paccayo ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:8.1: Pañcasu kho pana upādānakkhandhesu udayabbayānupassī viharati:
root_text.an8.2:8.2: ‘iti rūpaṁ, iti rūpassa samudayo, iti rūpassa atthaṅgamo;
root_text.an8.2:8.3: iti vedanā, iti vedanāya samudayo, iti vedanāya atthaṅgamo;
root_text.an8.2:8.6: iti viññāṇaṁ, iti viññāṇassa samudayo, iti viññāṇassa atthaṅgamo’ti.
root_text.an8.2:8.7: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, aṭṭhamo hetu aṭṭhamo paccayo ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:9.2: ‘ayaṁ kho āyasmā satthāraṁ upanissāya viharati aññataraṁ vā garuṭṭhāniyaṁ sabrahmacāriṁ, yatthassa tibbaṁ hirottappaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti pemañca gāravo ca.
root_text.an8.2:9.3: Addhā ayamāyasmā jānaṁ jānāti passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.an8.2:9.4: Ayampi dhammo piyattāya garuttāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:10.1: ‘Taṁ kho panāyamāyasmā satthāraṁ upanissāya viharanto aññataraṁ vā garuṭṭhāniyaṁ sabrahmacāriṁ, yatthassa tibbaṁ hirottappaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti pemañca gāravo ca, te kālena kālaṁ upasaṅkamitvā paripucchati paripañhati:
root_text.an8.2:10.3: Tassa te āyasmanto avivaṭañceva vivaranti, anuttānīkatañca uttānīkaronti, anekavihitesu ca kaṅkhāṭhāniyesu dhammesu kaṅkhaṁ paṭivinodenti.
root_text.an8.2:10.4: Addhā ayamāyasmā jānaṁ jānāti passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.an8.2:10.5: Ayampi dhammo piyattāya garuttāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:11.1: ‘Taṁ kho panāyamāyasmā dhammaṁ sutvā dvayena vūpakāsena sampādeti—kāyavūpakāsena ca cittavūpakāsena ca.
root_text.an8.2:11.2: Addhā ayamāyasmā jānaṁ jānāti passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.an8.2:11.3: Ayampi dhammo piyattāya garuttāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:12.1: ‘Sīlavā kho panāyamāyasmā pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī, samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu.
root_text.an8.2:12.2: Addhā ayamāyasmā jānaṁ jānāti passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.an8.2:12.3: Ayampi dhammo piyattāya garuttāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:13.1: ‘Bahussuto kho panāyamāyasmā sutadharo sutasannicayo. Ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpāssa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an8.2:13.2: Addhā ayamāyasmā jānaṁ jānāti passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.an8.2:13.3: Ayampi dhammo piyattāya garuttāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:14.1: ‘Āraddhavīriyo kho panāyamāyasmā viharati akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ upasampadāya, thāmavā daḷhaparakkamo anikkhittadhuro kusalesu dhammesu.
root_text.an8.2:14.2: Addhā ayamāyasmā jānaṁ jānāti passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.an8.2:14.3: Ayampi dhammo piyattāya garuttāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:15.2: Sāmaṁ vā dhammaṁ bhāsati paraṁ vā ajjhesati ariyaṁ vā tuṇhībhāvaṁ nātimaññati.
root_text.an8.2:15.3: Addhā ayamāyasmā jānaṁ jānāti passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.an8.2:15.4: Ayampi dhammo piyattāya garuttāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:16.2: iti rūpaṁ, iti rūpassa samudayo, iti rūpassa atthaṅgamo;
root_text.an8.2:16.6: iti viññāṇaṁ, iti viññāṇassa samudayo, iti viññāṇassa atthaṅgamoti.
root_text.an8.2:16.7: Addhā ayamāyasmā jānaṁ jānāti passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.an8.2:16.8: Ayampi dhammo piyattāya garuttāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an8.2:17.1: Ime kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭha hetū aṭṭha paccayā ādibrahmacariyikāya paññāya appaṭiladdhāya paṭilābhāya, paṭiladdhāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā saṁvattantī”ti.

translation_text.an8.2:1.1: “Mendicants, there are eight causes and reasons that lead to acquiring the wisdom fundamental to the spiritual life, and to its increase, growth, and full development once it has been acquired.
translation_text.an8.2:1.3: It’s when a mendicant lives relying on the Teacher or a spiritual companion in a teacher’s role. And they set up a keen sense of conscience and prudence for them, with warmth and respect.
translation_text.an8.2:2.1: When a mendicant lives relying on the Teacher or a spiritual companion in a teacher’s role—with a keen sense of conscience and prudence for them, with warmth and respect—from time to time they go and ask them questions:
translation_text.an8.2:2.2: ‘Why, sir, does it say this? What does that mean?’
translation_text.an8.2:2.3: Those venerables reveal what is hidden, clarify what is unclear, and dispel doubt regarding the many doubtful matters.
translation_text.an8.2:3.1: After hearing that teaching they perfect withdrawal of both body and mind.
translation_text.an8.2:4.1: A mendicant is ethical, restrained in the monastic code, conducting themselves well and resorting for alms in suitable places. Seeing danger in the slightest fault, they keep the rules they’ve undertaken.
translation_text.an8.2:5.1: They’re very learned, remembering and keeping what they’ve learned. These teachings are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased, describing a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure. They are very learned in such teachings, remembering them, rehearsing them, mentally scrutinizing them, and penetrating them theoretically.
translation_text.an8.2:6.1: They live with energy roused up for giving up unskillful qualities and embracing skillful qualities. They’re strong, staunchly vigorous, not slacking off when it comes to developing skillful qualities.
translation_text.an8.2:7.1: When in the Saṅgha they don’t engage in motley talk or low talk.
translation_text.an8.2:7.2: Either they talk on Dhamma, or they invite someone else to do so, or they respect noble silence.
translation_text.an8.2:8.1: They meditate observing rise and fall in the five grasping aggregates.
translation_text.an8.2:8.2: ‘Such is form, such is the origin of form, such is the disappearance of form.
translation_text.an8.2:8.3: Such is feeling, such is the origin of feeling, such is the disappearance of feeling.
translation_text.an8.2:8.4: Such is perception, such is the origin of perception, such is the ending of perception.
translation_text.an8.2:8.5: Such are choices, such is the origin of choices, such is the ending of choices.
translation_text.an8.2:8.6: Such is consciousness, such is the origin of consciousness, such is the disappearance of consciousness.’
translation_text.an8.2:9.2: ‘This venerable lives relying on the Teacher or a spiritual companion in a teacher’s role. They set up a keen sense of conscience and prudence for them, with warmth and respect.
translation_text.an8.2:9.3: Clearly this venerable knows and sees.’
translation_text.an8.2:9.4: This quality leads to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an8.2:10.1: ‘This venerable lives relying on the Teacher or a spiritual companion in a teacher’s role, and from time to time they go and ask them questions …
translation_text.an8.2:10.4: Clearly this venerable knows and sees.’
translation_text.an8.2:10.5: This quality also leads to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an8.2:11.1: ‘After hearing that teaching they perfect withdrawal of both body and mind.
translation_text.an8.2:11.2: Clearly this venerable knows and sees.’
translation_text.an8.2:11.3: This quality also leads to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an8.2:12.2: Clearly this venerable knows and sees.’
translation_text.an8.2:12.3: This quality also leads to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an8.2:13.1: ‘This venerable is very learned, remembering and keeping what they’ve learned. …
translation_text.an8.2:13.2: Clearly this venerable knows and sees.’
translation_text.an8.2:13.3: This quality also leads to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an8.2:14.1: ‘This venerable lives with energy roused up …
translation_text.an8.2:14.2: Clearly this venerable knows and sees.’
translation_text.an8.2:14.3: This quality also leads to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an8.2:15.1: ‘When in the Saṅgha they don’t engage in motley talk or low talk.
translation_text.an8.2:15.2: Either they talk on Dhamma, or they invite someone else to do so, or they respect noble silence.
translation_text.an8.2:15.3: Clearly this venerable knows and sees.’
translation_text.an8.2:15.4: This quality also leads to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an8.2:16.1: ‘They meditate observing rise and fall in the five grasping aggregates. …
translation_text.an8.2:16.7: Clearly this venerable knows and sees.’
translation_text.an8.2:16.8: This quality also leads to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an8.2:17.1: These are the eight causes and reasons that lead to acquiring the wisdom fundamental to the spiritual life, and to its increase, growth, and full development once it has been acquired.”

variant_text.an8.2:5.1: sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ → satthā sabyañjanā (si); sātthaṁ savyañjanaṁ (pts1ed) | dhātā → dhatā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
